header.stance: 儘管通膨訊號轉弱，實質殖利率主導的美債拋售仍使殖利率曲線趨陡

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場主論點

7月1日收盤延續前一交易日的美債拋售，但其內部訊息已經改變。10 年期殖利率上升4個基點至 4.48%，其實質殖利率上升5個基點至 2.25%，損益兩平通膨率則下滑1個基點至 2.23%。因此，近期通膨壓力確有緩解，卻未轉化為名目殖利率下降。最清晰的解讀是，實質利率與期限溢價的門檻升高：美國製造業展現韌性、溝通減少的聯準會帶來不確定性，以及長端供給與買方基礎疑慮持續存在，均蓋過油價走低所帶來的通膨降溫動能。

這樣的組合代表市場較 6月30日出現重要演變。週二的拋售受到股市走強、職缺增加及消費者信心改善所強化。到了週三，半導體類股劇烈反轉、原油再度下跌，且歐元區通膨走軟；這些條件通常能為存續期間提供更多支撐。美債卻仍然走弱，長債表現落後 2 年期債券。Fed 主席 Kevin Warsh 表示通膨風險已經消退，使政策敏感券種的殖利率降至盤中低點；但他拒絕對下一次決策提供指引，令未來路徑取決於每一次數據發布。較安靜的溝通機制可以降低市場對立即升息的焦慮，同時也提高殖利率曲線較遠端所要求的不確定性補償。

信用市場與全球利率也呼應了當前通膨緩解與長期融資信心之間的分野。公司債基金同時受到基準存續期間與風險資產拖累而走弱，儘管保險公司仍持續承接與 AI 基礎設施相關的大型私募債券。Brightline 即將進行的重整與 Trans-Oil 債券突然重新定價顯示，融資管道對債權人控制權、流動性及新聞事件風險仍極為敏感。

在歐洲，通膨下降降低 ECB 再度行動的急迫性，但未終結薪資與第2輪效應的爭論。隨後的亞洲發展則透過 BOJ 資產負債表收縮、日圓疲弱及韓國通膨穩固，帶來不同的限制。因此，主要收盤論點是在通膨背景轉弱之下，由實質殖利率主導的熊市趨陡；政策不透明及個別信用壓力則使存續期間成本維持高檔。

## 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美債跌勢遍及整條殖利率曲線，且以最長端跌幅最大。2 年期殖利率上升3個基點至 4.17%，5 年期上升5個基點至 4.24%，10 年期增加4個基點至 4.48%，30 年期則攀升6個基點至 4.97%。此一型態延續週二的全面走弱，但當日領跌者已不再是基準 10 年期債券。即使政策敏感的 2 年期債券在盤中對 Warsh的言論作出正面反應，長存續期間所需補償仍升幅最大。

殖利率曲線斜率清楚呈現不同期限的差異。2s10s 利差擴大1個基點至 31，2s30s 擴大3個基點至 80。2s5s10s 蝶式利差上升3個基點至-17，因 5 年期債券相對於 2 年期與 10 年期兩翼，回吐部分先前的偏貴程度。2s10s30s 蝶式利差下降1個基點至-18，使 10 年期債券相對於 2 年期與長債平均值仍然偏貴。當日走勢既反映政策與成長交會處所承受的壓力，也包含 30 年期更大幅度的去化調整。

通膨連結債券顯示，名目債券的拋售幾乎完全由實質因素主導。5 年期與 7 年期實質殖利率均上升5個基點，分別至 1.98% 與 2.11%。10 年期實質殖利率也上升5個基點，20 年期與 30 年期實質殖利率則分別增加4個與5個基點，至 2.58% 與 2.78%。在實質利率近乎平行上移之際，10 年期損益兩平通膨率下滑1個基點，意味當前通膨補償完全未吸收名目殖利率的升幅。這是在通膨讀數略為降溫的環境下，成長、政策及存續期間風險門檻同步升高。

美國經濟活動證據展現韌性，但並非全面強勁。6月製造業連續第6個月擴張，戰爭所驅動的投入成本飆升有所緩解，使產出訊號維持正面，卻未延續先前的通膨動能。5月營建支出上升 0.1%，換算年率為 $2.2102兆，但仍較1年前低 1.5%。住宅工程增加 0.3%、公共建設增加 0.5%，抵銷民間非住宅支出下降 0.3%。相較於資本需求廣泛再加速，這項組合更能支持經濟活動持續。

勞動市場資訊也不支持簡單的鷹派結論。紐約聯準銀行的 5月勞動市場緊俏指數由修正後的-0.07 微升至-0.02，因離職人數與每名有效求職者對應的職缺數均溫和增加。其相關的3個月 ECI 薪資成長訊號為 0.74%，年化為 2.96%，仍略低於該指標標準化後的長期均值。繼週二的職缺數據穩健後，這次回升使勞動市場閒置程度看來並未突然擴大，但隱含薪資增速的威脅，仍低於地區調查中的物價與就業組合。

Warsh 在 Sintra的談話改變了盤中走勢，卻未決定收盤結果。他表示，在此前4週內，通膨預期與通膨風險均已下降，2 年期殖利率因而跌至約 4.15%的盤中低點。他也重申恢復物價穩定的承諾，但拒絕暗示 7月是否會升息。FOMC 在 6月將目標利率維持於 3.50%-3.75%，18 份預測中有9份顯示今年會升息，投資人仍定價年底前至少升息1次 25 個基點。當逐次會議的溝通資訊含量下降時，市場認為立即行動的機率降低，仍可與較高的不確定性溢價同時存在。

溝通轉變目前仍屬推論，尚未成為完整的重新定價機制。自 Warsh 在 6月首度參加 FOMC 以來，Fed 官員已公開露面 12 次，低於過去10年同期平均的 18 次。分析師預期，訊號減少將提高美債波動率與期限溢價，但1項廣受關注的債券波動率指標，自 6月會議以來仍相對穩定。週三的長端跌勢符合這項疑慮，但後續數據與實際溝通頻率仍須證明此現象是否會持續。

Fed funds 部位顯示，機率仍低的7月升息尾部風險已開始吸引資金。利率交換隱含約9個基點的25個基點升息幅度，相當於約36%的機率；在 Warsh 強調物價穩定之前，這項機率接近0。追蹤7月29日決策的8月 Fed funds 期貨未平倉量，由6月會議前約454,000口升至週一接近590,000口，增幅約30%。前端不確定性上升，同時伴隨7個月來最看多的美債直接部位及長端相對勝出的需求，符合近期政策風險升高、後續週期走平的組合。

能源市場為損益兩平通膨率下滑提供了可信理由。美國官員稱與伊朗的間接會談具建設性，且荷姆茲海峽仍維持開放，WTI 與 Brent 因而再度下跌。EIA 報告則提供相反力量：截至 6月26日當週，商業原油庫存減少 380萬桶，汽油庫存下降 230萬，餾分油庫存則增加 250萬。煉油廠產能利用率升至 96.6%，戰略石油儲備減少 550萬桶。實體庫存收緊仍未扭轉期貨市場因和平前景而出現的較大幅回落，使實質殖利率成為美債拋售的主要承擔因素。

國庫券走勢使最短現金券種與標售券種分化。4 週、6 週及 8 週期國庫券的附息債等值殖利率分別下降4個、3個及4個基點，至 3.63%、3.70% 及 3.69%。13 週期殖利率下降3個基點至 3.80%，26 週期下降1個基點至 3.97%，52 週期則上升1個基點至 3.98%。17 週期殖利率上升1個基點至 3.89%，使當日走弱集中於週三出售的期限，而非整條國庫券殖利率曲線。

美國財政部的 $720億、119天期標售以 3.795%的最高得標利率決標，投標倍數為 2.66，最高利率配售比例為 57.66%。競爭性得標額包括間接投標人 $388.38億、直接投標人 $46.73億，以及初級交易商 $268.69億。該國庫券的 CUSIP 為 912797VP9，於 7月7日發行並交割，11月3日到期。週三的標售結果確定了此次發行的配售情況；7月7日的現金移轉則仍是後續融資事件。

因此，收盤延續 6月30日已可見的拋售，但部分風險偏好解釋已被取代。油價下跌、損益兩平通膨率下降、整體股市溫和走弱及晶片股重挫，均未促成美債反彈。各期限實質殖利率皆上升，30 年期債券的跌勢也使殖利率曲線由前端以外進一步趨陡。經濟活動具韌性與 Fed 依數據決策可解釋部分走勢；但指引減少及長端去化利率，仍是通膨緩解未能轉化為存續期間壓力減輕的可信競爭性解釋。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

上市公司債券報酬在各品質層級均走弱。LQD 下跌 0.56% 至 $108.46，HYG 下跌 0.48% 至 $79.59，JNK 則下跌 0.51% 至 $95.88。美債較大幅度的拋售，對較高評級債券造成存續期間拖累；科技類股主導的股市下跌則壓抑較低品質風險。整體方向大致相同，並未消除發行人分化：個別債券價格與融資結構的差異，遠比整體基金走勢更加顯著。

AI 基礎設施持續透過私募債券吸引大規模的耐心資本。截至 5月，發行量約達 $810億，創下自 2016 年開始統計以來同期最高。資料中心營運商 IREN 在 5月私募約 $21億，Blackstone 支持的 QTS 已募集 $8億，並計畫進一步融資。人壽保險公司是自然買方，因長天期、高評級的私募債務可與數十年期年金負債匹配；產業去年年金銷售額約達 $4,640億。

個別融資案的規模正在改變市場承接問題。為 Broadcom 與 Anthropic 基礎設施擴張籌措的 $350億融資案，起初採用傳統私募形式，預計最終將依 Rule 144A 方式交易。經協商決定的幣別、期限與結構可為借款人提供彈性，投資人則可尋求非流動性溢價。此管道將融資能力擴展到註冊債券以外，但隨著公開市場面臨飽和疑慮，也使更多 AI 曝險進入透明度有限、條款日益複雜的證券。

Brightline 則處於信用光譜的另一端。這家 Florida 鐵路公司原定於 7月1日支付公司債與高順位市政債券的票息，據報其準備金足以完成付款。但付款日並未解決圍繞 $55億債務的協商。持有 $12億公司債多數部位的避險基金，準備否決1項不賦予其控制權的方案；為 $11億免稅債券提供擔保的 Assured Guaranty，則已提供臨時營運貸款。

這場重整爭議不僅跨越資本結構，也涉及不同類型的債權人。市政債投資人正考慮提供超高順位融資，可能藉此取得所有權；Fortress 則準備放棄控制權。債權人仍希望在任何聲請前先達成協議方案，以限制訴訟成本，並避免涉及獨立受託人的特殊鐵路程序。因此，Brightline的情況是近期票息具備足夠流動性，但控制權、順位及營運融資問題仍未解決。這些是債務協商風險，並非預定付款本身已經違約的證據。

Trans-Oil 顯示新聞事件風險能多快衝擊原本正常履約的債券。其 $3億、2031 年到期債券報價約為每一美元面額 70 美分，較週二開盤下跌約 21 points；其 $4.5億、2029 年到期債券也在 70 左右。這波下跌發生於烏克蘭媒體報導之後，促使投資人要求與公司會面。1名 ING 分析師預期基本面影響有限，使債券市場的立即重新定價與 Moldova、羅馬尼亞及塞爾維亞業務尚待釐清的評估之間，出現巨大落差。

因此，信用管道廣泛暢通，但附帶條件。保險公司的需求足以承接數十億美元規模的 AI 融資，然而這項能力取決於結構、期限及非流動性補償。Brightline的近期票息資源並未解決潛在重整中的債權人控制權，Trans-Oil 債券也在基本面爭議釐清前即已重新定價。整體基金下跌反映存續期間與風險均面臨艱難的1天；個別發行人的結果仍由現金流、債權順位、透明度及融資計畫的可信度決定。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐元區通膨帶來已開發市場最強烈的通膨降溫訊號。6月消費者物價較1年前上升 2.8%，低於 5月的 3.2%，也低於 3.0%的預估中位數。核心通膨降幅也超出預期，服務業通膨則降至 3.2%。德國 10 年期殖利率回吐早前部分升幅，收盤仍上升2個基點至 2.88%。德債獲得部分支撐，但殖利率仍然上升，顯示單一份較軟的數據並未消除政策與期限溢價的底部支撐。

ECB的爭論由是否需要立即行動，轉向能源價格反轉能否持續。Lagarde 表示，在油價回到戰前水準附近後，通膨上行與成長下行風險已更趨平衡。Stournaras 稱通膨下降是重大的下行意外，並認為若無劇烈變化，7月幾乎沒有行動理由。Rehn 與 Kocher 也認為近期第2輪效應的風險已經降低。Kaasik 仍認為至少再升息1次是合理的，Lane 則強調通膨傳導至薪資、食品與服務業存在時滯。

這項分歧之所以重要，是因 ECB 已在 6月將政策利率調升至 2.25%，其預測顯示通膨直到 2027 年仍將長時間高於 2%。7月看來不太可能採取行動，9月因有更多薪資數據可供評估，可能成為較合理的爭論時點。油價下降與當前通膨轉弱，可降低歐元前端利率及美債損益兩平通膨率的壓力。若薪資或服務業壓力持續，即使能源整體通膨維持低迷，全球政策利率底部仍將較高。

歐洲結果也為美國實質殖利率拋售增添限制條件。同步通膨衝擊正逐步消退，但美債實質殖利率上升，德債殖利率收盤也走高。此一背離顯示，全球存續期間的反應不僅受到當前物價數據影響。國內成長韌性、政策溝通及主權債券去化利率，對實質補償仍有更大影響；歐洲通膨下降則主要壓抑名目殖利率中的通膨成分。

在紐約截止時間後，日本呈現相反的政策張力。日圓報價約為每美元 162.73，市場擔憂 1%的 BOJ 政策利率與漸進正常化，不足以應對通膨、寬廣的國際利差及財政擴張。1年期外匯選擇權賦予美元兌日圓升至 180的機率為 15%，但市場並不認為這項極端結果迫在眉睫。當局於 4月28日至 5月27日期間投入創紀錄的 ¥11.73兆，顯示干預風險仍然重大，並將匯率防衛與外匯存底管理連結起來。

美國收盤後公布的日本6月 Tankan，增添另一項正常化訊號。大型製造業者信心由17升至22，創2018年以來最高；企業預期5年後年通膨為2.6%，亦為2014年有資料以來最高。調查同時呈現企業景氣轉強與長期物價預期創高，隔夜利率交換則賦予 BOJ 在10月前再次升息58%的機率。其公布時間使它無法解釋週三美債拋售，卻提高下一交易日 JGB 與日圓的政策門檻，也強化不能僅從油價下跌推論區域通膨降溫的理由。

6月貨幣基數報告於下午7:50 ET 公布，時間已在完整美國收盤之後，因此是影響下一個亞洲存續期間交易環境的資訊。貨幣基數較1年前收縮 13.7%，快於 5月的 12.2% 降幅，因 BOJ 活期帳戶餘額下降 16.4%。經季節調整後，月度收縮速度折合年率為 22.9%。即使弱勢貨幣持續對政策利率路徑構成壓力，資產負債表流動性仍在撤出；此一組合可能使國內殖利率曲線趨陡，並改變海外債券的相對吸引力。

南韓也帶來另一項稍晚出現的亞洲限制。6月消費者通膨由 3.1% 加速至 3.2%，符合預估中位數；核心通膨則維持在 2.5%。半導體出口接近成長3倍，經工作日調整的出口增幅接近 60%，使 BOK 在 7月16日會議前對需求面的擔憂升高。這項數據在美國收盤後公布，因此並未造成美債走勢。它與 Tankan 共同強化了區域性型態：AI 投資、貨幣疲弱及國內通膨，均限制全球油價下跌能讓政策利率下降的幅度。

因此，全球存續期間最終面臨2股相互競爭的力量。歐洲提供已實現的通膨降溫，並降低近期緊縮的急迫性。日本與南韓則提供稍晚出現的企業需求轉強、流動性撤出、匯率壓力及國內通膨具韌性的證據。美國殖利率曲線的實質殖利率升高且長端趨陡，符合共同能源衝擊正在消退，但各央行反應函數、財政路徑及跨境買方誘因仍遠非一致的全球環境。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

隨著美國實質殖利率與美元上升，新興市場債券走弱。EMB 下跌 0.44% 至 $96.02，美元指數上升 0.20% 至 101.39。1項盤中新興市場貨幣指標下跌 0.2%，抹去當時 2026 年以來的漲幅。即使油價下降減輕許多主權國家的進口成本，此一組合仍使外部金融條件收緊。整體基金走勢同時反映美債存續期間與國家風險，因此更具資訊性的訊號來自匯率及個別融資計畫。

在 Warsh 發言前，亞洲貨幣領跌。由於外國投資人淨賣出 1.46兆韓元、即 $9.38億的股票，South Korean 韓元逼近 2009 年以來最低水準。稍後公布的韓國通膨數據，進一步提供國內政策理由，使貨幣與利率市場持續保持敏感。美國實質利率路徑走高，提高本地資產的報酬門檻；韓國通膨持續，則限制 BOK 以更寬鬆政策抗衡貨幣弱勢的空間。

南非顯示主權與次主權融資風險可能分化。中央政府計畫在財政年度融資中增發現有蘭特-denominated sukuk，利用本地市場，而非新增硬通貨債權。相較之下，約翰尼斯堡在 7月1日開始的年度面臨估計達 21億蘭特的未融資預算缺口。National 美國財政部表示，該市收入預測相對於經審計結果似乎過度樂觀，並敦促加強徵收與合理化支出。市政缺口屬於地方債務管理問題，與主權 sukuk 融資有別。

因此，本交易日並未因能源價格下跌而為新興市場帶來一致利多。美元走強、美債實質殖利率升高及股票資金外流，均壓抑外部流動性；國內通膨與財政可信度則決定各國表現。南非仍可使用本幣債務管道，但約翰尼斯堡的預算計算顯示，即使中央政府融資持續，收入執行仍可能限制次主權實體。主要傳導機制仍是匯率與再融資成本，而 EMB 下跌並不意味現金債券利差有廣泛一致的結論。

## 跨資產

股票市場帶來集中的成長與估值衝擊。S&P 500 下跌 0.22% 至 7,483.23，但 Nasdaq 100 下跌 1.54%，費城半導體指數更重挫 6.27%。晶片股在第2季強勁上漲後下跌，市場也重新檢視 AI 相關估值與資本支出。整體股市並未釋放衰退訊號，但與投資熱潮關係最密切的產業出現劇烈反轉。美債未能同步上漲，使本交易日有別於傳統的避險走勢。

波動率確認這波拋售仍然集中。VIX 僅上升 0.85% 至 16.59，且1週內仍下跌近 11%。投資人反映更多分化與科技風險，卻未為系統性股市衝擊支付溢價。此一型態有助解釋半導體類股疲弱為何能與實質殖利率上升並存：重新定價質疑 AI 報酬的分布，卻未證明需求或金融條件全面崩潰。

商品走勢偏向通膨降溫。WTI 下跌 1.32% 至 $68.58，Brent 下跌 1.85% 至 $71.57，使兩者1個月內跌幅均超過 21%。美國與伊朗的接觸具建設性，且石油持續流通，蓋過美國單週庫存下降的影響。黃金則反向上漲 1.13% 至 $4,068.30，但1個月內仍下跌 10.79%。金價單日上漲顯示部分避險需求，但較長期間的跌勢仍與實質殖利率處於高檔相符。

儘管油價下降且科技股走弱，美元仍然走強，使跨境融資環境收緊。因此，跨資產走勢使利率變動更為複雜，而非予以確認。原油與歐元區通膨支持損益兩平通膨率下降，晶片股拋售則削弱近期風險偏好。整體股市穩定、波動率低迷及美元走強，使美債未能獲得強勁的避險買盤。名目殖利率的剩餘升幅集中於實質補償，使成長韌性、政策不確定性及長端去化壓力，成為更持久的固定收益傳導管道。

## 市場前瞻意義

因假期提前至 7月2日公布的美國就業數據，是對實質殖利率主導之拋售最接近的考驗。6月非農就業與截至 6月27日當週的失業救濟申請數將於上午8:30 ET 公布，5月工廠訂單則於 10:00 公布，均早於建議的下午2:00 固定收益收盤時間。招聘穩健、薪資壓力及訂單具韌性，將延續製造業訊號，使 2 年期至 10 年期實質殖利率持續承受上行壓力，並確認週三的油價走低與損益兩平通膨率下降，本身不足以降低政策門檻。若就業轉弱且訂單疲軟，將挑戰以成長解釋拋售的論點，並恢復殖利率曲線中段存續期間獲得支撐的可能。由於交易時段縮短，判斷走勢反映政策重新定價或假期流動性清淡時，殖利率曲線反應能否持續，比初始方向更為重要。

當 4 週期與 8 週期國庫券分別以 $850億規模於 7月2日、上午11:30 ET 標售時，美國財政部現金需求將面臨另一項考驗。2批國庫券皆於 7月7日交割，與週三的 $720億、119天期國庫券相同。參與廣泛、短天期國庫券殖利率穩定及交易商得標額受控，將確認目前 4 週至 13 週期現金券種的漲勢，足以吸收假期期間的合併交割。若國庫券突然走弱、交易商留倉明顯增加或擔保融資承壓，將挑戰此一解讀，並再次引發季末時已出現的資產負債表疑慮。第一波傳導將經由國庫券與擔保品融資發生；若擾動持續，則可能在縮短的交易時段內進一步降低附息債市場流動性。

7月2日的瑞士 6月 CPI 於上午2:30 ET 公布，歐元區 5月失業率則於 5:00 公布，將成為歐洲對低通膨論點的下一項檢驗。瑞士物價偏軟、歐元區勞動市場閒置程度增加，以及油價維持在戰後區間附近，將強化週三的歐元區 2.8% 數據、降低歐洲前端利率壓力，並確認能源衝擊的廣度正在縮小。若通膨穩固、勞動市場更為緊俏，或美國與伊朗的談判再度受阻，將挑戰 ECB 延長暫停期的論點，並透過德債殖利率、歐元通膨補償及美債損益兩平通膨率傳導。結果也將決定能源價格下降能否緩解依賴進口的新興市場所面臨的美元融資壓力，抑或相對利率對美元的支撐仍是主導全球的管道。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為基準。

overall_summary.headline: 實質殖利率與長端去化壓力蓋過通膨訊號轉弱
overall_summary.session_narrative: 本次完整紐約收盤呈現實質殖利率主導的熊市趨陡，而非通膨主導的拋售。油價走軟、歐元區通膨下降及半導體類股劇烈反轉，仍未帶動避險買盤，原因是美國經濟活動展現韌性、Fed 指引資訊含量降低，以及長端供給疑慮推升了必要補償。個別發行人壓力與美元走強限制了更廣泛的風險緩解，而波動率低迷則使這波走勢未演變為系統性的風險逃離。
overall_summary.summary: 美債殖利率曲線全面走弱，實質殖利率上升與長端表現落後，蓋過損益兩平通膨率及油價下跌的影響。經濟活動展現韌性，加上 Fed 減少政策指引，使存續期間的門檻維持高檔；信用市場雖仍暢通，選擇性卻極高。

section_summaries.market_read: 儘管通膨投入訊號降溫，收盤仍推高實質利率與期限溢價門檻。
section_summaries.next_check: 長端表現持續落後與否，將顯示較高的去化溢價能否延續。
section_summaries.summary: 即使油價與損益兩平通膨率走軟，實質殖利率及長端補償仍推動美債再度遭到拋售。經濟活動展現韌性、Fed 減少指引及買方基礎疑慮持續存在，蓋過通膨降溫動能；個別信用壓力亦進一步顯示，近期通膨緩解尚未恢復市場對融資條件能夠持久穩定的信心。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線趨陡，使這波拋售的重心由政策風險轉向存續期間風險。
section_summaries.next_check: 就業數據與 Fed 實際溝通頻率，必須確認這項溢價能否持續。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 美債跌勢廣泛但以長債領跌，實質殖利率上升、損益兩平通膨率下滑。Fed funds 期貨顯示可交易的7月升息尾部風險，同時投資人建立7個月來最看多的美債直接部位，使近期政策不確定性與後續長端相對勝出需求並存。

section_summaries.market_read: 信用管道仍然暢通，但債權順位與流動性的重要性高於 beta。
section_summaries.next_check: Brightline的控制權協商及 Trans-Oil的營運評估，是關鍵的釐清重點。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 公司債基金受存續期間與科技風險拖累而走弱，但民間 AI 基礎設施債務仍持續吸引保險公司需求。廣泛的融資能力仍取決於結構與透明度，Brightline 尚未解決的債權人控制權爭議，以及 Trans-Oil 受新聞事件驅動的重新定價，均凸顯發行人層級的顯著分化。

section_summaries.market_read: 全球通膨降溫抑制了通膨補償，但未能為存續期間帶來廣泛緩解。
section_summaries.next_check: 歐元區薪資與收盤後公布的 Tankan，必須顯示通膨降溫能否克服亞洲正常化壓力。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐元區通膨下降，降低 ECB 立即行動的壓力；但收盤後公布的 Tankan 顯示，日本大型製造業信心創2018年來最高，5年期企業通膨預期亦創紀錄。能源通膨消退可抑制損益兩平通膨率，卻無法消除亞洲正常化與全球期限溢價底部。

section_summaries.market_read: 美元走強，使匯率與再融資管道成為新興市場的主要傳導機制。
section_summaries.next_check: 在融資壓力緩解前，匯率穩定度與市政收入執行情況必須改善。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 儘管油價下跌，美國實質殖利率上升與美元走強仍使外部條件收緊，拖累新興市場債券。匯率壓力、股票資金外流及財政可信度不一，使各國表現持續分化；本地融資管道也無法消除再融資及收入執行風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產走勢削弱殖利率因通膨而上升的論點，卻幾乎未帶來避險需求。
section_summaries.next_check: 更廣泛的波動率急升或石油供應再度受阻，將考驗實質殖利率主導的解讀。
section_summaries.summary: 半導體類股劇烈反轉、整體股市走軟，卻未引發美債避險買盤，波動率也維持低迷。原油下跌支撐損益兩平通膨率下滑，黃金則上漲；但美元走強及系統性風險情緒穩定，使較高的實質補償成為本交易日固定收益市場的主導管道。

section_summaries.market_read: 確認此一走勢需要殖利率曲線與融資反應持續，而非僅有初步的新聞事件波動。
section_summaries.next_check: 追蹤縮短交易時段的殖利率曲線、交易商得標額及歐洲利率反應，直到 7月2日。
section_summaries.summary: 7月2日的3項考驗將決定實質殖利率主導的拋售能否延續：因假期提前公布的美國就業數據、合併進行的國庫券標售與交割需求，以及歐洲通膨和勞動市場訊號。各項考驗將區分經濟成長韌性與政策重新定價，究竟有別於清淡交易時段的流動性因素，還是全球通膨動能再起。

watch_events.market_implication: 殖利率曲線反應若持續，將確認政策重新定價；若普遍轉弱，則恢復中段券種的利多。
watch_events.market_trigger: 招聘穩健、薪資壓力及訂單具韌性，將使實質殖利率持續承受上行壓力。
watch_events.title: 美國就業與工廠訂單考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察就業、薪資與訂單是否延續美國經濟活動展現韌性的訊號。

watch_events.market_implication: 融資壓力將先衝擊國庫券與擔保品，之後再損害附息債市場的流動性。
watch_events.market_trigger: 國庫券突然走弱、交易商留倉增加或擔保融資承壓，均顯示市場吃緊。
watch_events.title: 假期國庫券標售與交割需求
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察合併交割前後的參與程度、交易商得標額與擔保融資。

watch_events.market_implication: 歐洲前端利率與美債損益兩平通膨率下降，減輕美元融資壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 物價偏軟、勞動市場閒置程度增加及油價穩定，將確認能源衝擊正在消退。
watch_events.title: 歐洲通膨降溫的延續情況
watch_events.watch_line: 觀察瑞士物價、歐元區勞動市場閒置程度及油價，確認通膨降溫範圍是否持續擴大。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 1 close extended the prior session’s Treasury selloff, yet its internal message changed. The 10-year yield rose 4 basis points to 4.48% even though its real yield increased 5 basis points to 2.25% and its breakeven slipped 1 basis point to 2.23%. Near-term inflation relief was therefore present, but it did not translate into lower nominal yields. The cleanest interpretation is a higher real-rate and term-premium hurdle: resilient US manufacturing, uncertainty around a less communicative Federal Reserve and persistent long-end supply and buyer-base concerns outweighed the disinflationary impulse from cheaper oil.

That mix marks an important evolution from June 30. Tuesday’s selloff was reinforced by stronger equities, job openings and consumer confidence. Wednesday brought a sharp semiconductor reversal, another decline in crude and softer euro-area inflation, conditions that ordinarily give duration more support. Treasuries still cheapened, with the long bond underperforming the 2-year. Fed Chair Kevin Warsh’s statement that inflation risks had receded pulled the policy-sensitive sector to its intraday low, but his refusal to guide the next decision left the future path dependent on each data release. A quieter communications regime can lower immediate hike anxiety and simultaneously increase the compensation demanded for uncertainty farther along the curve.

Credit and global rates echoed that separation between current inflation relief and durable financing confidence. Corporate-bond funds weakened with both benchmark duration and risk assets, even as insurers continued to absorb large private placements tied to AI infrastructure. Brightline’s looming restructuring and Trans-Oil’s abrupt bond repricing showed that access remained highly sensitive to creditor control, liquidity and headline risk.

In Europe, lower inflation reduced the urgency of another ECB move without ending the debate over wages and second-round effects. Later Asian developments added a different constraint through BOJ balance-sheet contraction, yen weakness and firm Korean inflation. The primary close thesis is consequently a real-yield-led bear steepening inside a softer inflation backdrop, with policy opacity and selective credit stress keeping the cost of duration elevated.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury losses covered the curve and were largest at the far end. The 2-year yield rose 3 basis points to 4.17%, the 5-year increased 5 to 4.24%, the 10-year added 4 to 4.48%, and the 30-year climbed 6 to 4.97%. The pattern continued Tuesday’s broad cheapening but shifted the daily leadership away from the benchmark 10-year. Long-duration compensation rose most even as the policy-sensitive 2-year reacted favorably to Warsh during the session.

Curve slopes made the maturity distinction explicit. The 2s10s spread widened 1 basis point to 31, and 2s30s widened 3 to 80. The 2s5s10s butterfly rose 3 basis points to -17 as the 5-year surrendered some of its earlier richness against the 2- and 10-year wings. The 2s10s30s butterfly declined 1 basis point to -18, leaving the 10-year relatively rich against the 2-year and long-bond average. The day combined pressure around the policy-growth intersection with a still-larger clearing adjustment at 30 years.

Inflation-linked securities showed that the nominal selloff was overwhelmingly real. 5- and 7-year real yields each rose 5 basis points, to 1.98% and 2.11%. The 10-year real yield also gained 5, and 20- and 30-year real yields increased 4 and 5 basis points, to 2.58% and 2.78%. Against that parallel real-rate increase, the 1-basis-point decline in the 10-year breakeven meant current inflation compensation absorbed none of the nominal rise. This was a higher growth, policy and duration-risk hurdle inside a modestly cooler inflation reading.

US activity evidence was resilient but not uniformly strong. Manufacturing expanded for a 6th consecutive month in June as the war-driven surge in input costs eased, preserving a positive output signal without extending the earlier inflation impulse. May construction spending rose 0.1% to a $2.2102 trillion annual rate, though it remained 1.5% below a year earlier. A 0.3% gain in residential work and 0.5% increase in public construction offset a 0.3% decline in private nonresidential spending. The mix supports continued activity more readily than a broad reacceleration in capital demand.

Labor information also resisted a simple hawkish conclusion. The New York Fed’s May labor-tightness index edged up to -0.02 from a revised -0.07 as quits and vacancies per effective searcher increased modestly. Its associated 3-month ECI wage-growth signal was 0.74%, or 2.96% annualized, still slightly below the indicator’s normalized long-run mean. After Tuesday’s firm job-openings data, the rebound kept labor slack from looking abrupt, but the implied wage pace was less threatening than the price and employment mix in the regional surveys.

Warsh’s Sintra remarks changed the intraday path without settling the close. He said inflation expectations and inflation risks had declined over the preceding 4 weeks, and 2-year yields fell to session lows near 4.15%. He also repeated the commitment to restore price stability and declined to signal whether July would bring a hike. The FOMC had held its target at 3.50%-3.75% in June, nine of 18 submitted projections showed an increase this year, and investors still priced at least 1 25-basis-point rise by year-end. A lower perceived probability of an immediate move can coexist with a higher uncertainty premium when meeting-by-meeting communication becomes less informative.

The communication shift remains an inference, not a completed repricing regime. Fed officials had made 12 public appearances since Warsh’s June FOMC debut, compared with an average of 18 over the same interval during the prior decade. Analysts expected fewer signals to produce more Treasury volatility and term premium, yet a widely watched bond-volatility gauge had remained relatively steady since the June meeting. Wednesday’s long-end loss is compatible with that concern, though upcoming data and actual communication frequency must establish whether it becomes persistent.

Fed-funds positioning showed that the low-probability July tail was nevertheless attracting capital. Swaps embedded about 9 basis points of a 25-basis-point increase, or roughly a 36% probability, after that likelihood had been near zero before Warsh’s price-stability emphasis. Open interest in August fed-funds futures, which reference the July 29 decision, rose from about 454,000 contracts before the June meeting to almost 590,000 by Monday, an increase near 30%. Rising front-end uncertainty therefore coexisted with the most bullish outright Treasury positioning in 7 months and demand for long-end outperformance, a combination consistent with near-term policy risk and a flatter expected cycle beyond it.

Energy markets gave breakevens a credible reason to fall. WTI and Brent declined again as US officials described indirect talks with Iran as constructive and the Strait of Hormuz remained open. The EIA release supplied a counterforce: commercial crude stocks fell 3.8 million barrels and gasoline inventories declined 2.3 million in the week ended June 26, while distillates rose 2.5 million. Refinery utilization increased to 96.6%, and the Strategic Petroleum Reserve fell 5.5 million barrels. Physical inventory tightening did not dislodge the larger peace-driven retreat in futures, leaving real yields to carry the Treasury selloff.

Bills separated the shortest cash maturities from the auction sector. 4-, 6- and 8-week coupon-equivalent yields fell 4, 3 and 4 basis points to 3.63%, 3.70% and 3.69%. The 13-week yield declined 3 basis points to 3.80%, the 26-week eased 1 to 3.97%, and the 52-week increased 1 to 3.98%. The 17-week yield rose 1 basis point to 3.89%, concentrating the daily cheapening around the maturity sold Wednesday rather than across the entire bill curve.

Treasury’s $72 billion 119-day auction stopped at a 3.795% high rate with 2.66 bid-to-cover and 57.66% allotted at the high. Competitive awards included $38.838 billion to indirect bidders, $4.673 billion to direct bidders and $26.869 billion to primary dealers. The bill, CUSIP 912797VP9, issues and settles July 7 and matures November 3. Wednesday’s auction result establishes the allocation of that offering; the July 7 cash transfer remains a later funding event.

The close therefore continued the selloff visible on June 30 but replaced part of its risk-on explanation. Falling oil, lower breakevens, mild broad-equity weakness and a severe chip-stock decline failed to produce a Treasury rally. Real yields rose across maturities, and the 30-year loss steepened the curve beyond the front end. Resilient activity and a data-dependent Fed account for part of the move; reduced guidance and the long-end clearing rate remain credible competing explanations for why inflation relief did not translate into duration relief.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Listed corporate-bond returns weakened across quality tiers. LQD fell 0.56% to $108.46, HYG declined 0.48% to $79.59, and JNK lost 0.51% to $95.88. The larger Treasury selloff imposed a duration drag on higher-grade bonds, while the technology-led equity decline weighed on lower-quality risk. The broadly similar direction did not erase issuer dispersion: direct bond prices and financing structures showed much sharper differences than the aggregate fund moves.

AI infrastructure continued to attract large pools of patient capital through private bonds. Issuance reached roughly $81 billion through May, the highest for that period in records beginning in 2016. Data-center operator IREN sold about $2.1 billion privately in May, and Blackstone-backed QTS had raised $800 million with plans for more. Life insurers are natural buyers because long-dated, high-grade private debt can match multi-decade annuity liabilities; industry annuity sales reached about $464 billion last year.

The scale of individual financings is changing the absorption problem. A $35 billion package for Broadcom and Anthropic’s infrastructure expansion began as a traditional private placement and is expected eventually to trade on a Rule 144A basis. Negotiated currency, maturity and structure give borrowers flexibility, and investors can seek an illiquidity premium. That channel broadens funding capacity beyond registered bonds, but it also moves more AI exposure into securities with limited transparency and increasingly complex terms as public markets confront saturation concerns.

Brightline sat at the opposite end of the credit spectrum. The Florida railway had coupons due July 1 on corporate and senior municipal bonds and was reported to have enough reserves to make them. The payment date did not resolve negotiations over a $5.5 billion debt pile. Hedge funds holding a majority of $1.2 billion in corporate debt were prepared to reject a plan that denied them control, while Assured Guaranty, which backs $1.1 billion of tax-exempt bonds, had provided stop-gap operating loans.

The restructuring contest crosses capital structures as well as creditor types. Municipal investors were considering super-senior financing that could give them ownership, and Fortress was prepared to relinquish control. Creditors still sought an agreed plan before any filing to limit court costs and avoid special railway procedures involving an independent trustee. Brightline therefore represented adequate liquidity for the immediate coupon alongside unresolved control, priority and operating-finance questions. Those are workout risks, not evidence that the scheduled payment itself had failed.

Trans-Oil showed how quickly headline risk could move an otherwise performing bond. Its $300 million 2031 note was quoted near 70 cents on the dollar, down about 21 points from Tuesday’s open, and its $450 million 2029 bond was also around 70. The fall followed a Ukrainian media report and prompted investors to seek meetings with the company. An ING analyst expected limited fundamental impact, leaving a wide gap between the bond market’s immediate repricing and the still-unresolved assessment of operations across Moldova, Romania and Serbia.

Credit access was consequently broad but conditional. Insurance demand could absorb multi-billion-dollar AI financings, yet that capacity depended on structure, maturity and compensation for illiquidity. Brightline’s near-term coupon resources did not settle creditor control over a prospective restructuring, and Trans-Oil’s bonds repriced before the fundamental dispute was resolved. Aggregate fund declines captured a difficult day for duration and risk; issuer-specific outcomes remained governed by cash flow, claim priority, transparency and the credibility of the financing plan.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Euro-area inflation delivered the strongest developed-market disinflation signal. Consumer prices rose 2.8% from a year earlier in June, down from 3.2% in May and below the 3.0% median estimate. Core inflation also eased more than expected, and services inflation fell to 3.2%. German 10-year yields pared an earlier rise to close 2 basis points higher at 2.88%. Bunds absorbed some relief, but the positive yield change showed that 1 softer release did not remove the policy and term-premium floor.

The ECB debate shifted from the need for immediate action to the durability of the energy reversal. Lagarde described upside inflation and downside growth risks as more broadly balanced after oil returned toward pre-war levels. Stournaras called the inflation decline a large downside surprise and saw little reason for July action absent a dramatic change. Rehn and Kocher also judged near-term second-round risks to have diminished. Kaasik still viewed at least 1 more increase as reasonable, and Lane emphasized delayed transmission into wages, food and services.

That disagreement matters because the ECB had raised its policy rate to 2.25% in June and its projections kept inflation above 2% well into 2027. July appeared unlikely to produce a move, leaving September as the more plausible point for a dispute after additional wage data. Lower oil and softer current inflation reduce the pressure on front-end euro rates and US breakevens. Persistent wage or services pressure would preserve a higher global policy floor even if headline energy inflation remains subdued.

The European result also qualified the US real-yield selloff. A synchronized inflation shock was fading at the margin, yet Treasury real yields rose and Bund yields finished higher. That divergence suggests global duration was responding to more than current price data. Domestic growth resilience, policy communication and sovereign clearing rates retained greater influence over real compensation, while lower European inflation mainly restrained the inflation component of nominal yields.

Japan presented the opposite policy tension after the New York cutoff. The yen was quoted around 162.73 per dollar amid concern that a 1% BOJ policy rate and gradual normalization were insufficient against inflation, a wide international rate gap and fiscal expansion. 1-year currency options assigned a 15% probability to dollar-yen reaching 180, though the extreme outcome was not viewed as imminent. Intervention risk remained material after authorities spent a record ¥11.73 trillion between April 28 and May 27, linking currency defense to the management of foreign reserves.

The June monetary-base release at 7:50 p.m. ET arrived after the completed US close and therefore informs the next Asian duration setting. The base contracted 13.7% from a year earlier, faster than May’s 12.2% decline, as BOJ current-account balances fell 16.4%. On a seasonally adjusted basis, the monthly contraction ran at a 22.9% annualized pace. Balance-sheet liquidity was being withdrawn even as the weak currency kept pressure on the policy-rate path, a combination that can steepen domestic curves and alter the relative attraction of overseas bonds.

Japan’s June Tankan added a separate normalization signal after the US close. Sentiment among large manufacturers rose to 22 from 17, its highest since 2018, and companies projected 2.6% annual inflation 5 years ahead, the highest reading in data extending to 2014. The survey therefore combined firmer corporate conditions with a record long-horizon price expectation, while overnight swaps assigned a 58% probability to another BOJ increase by October. Its timing prevents it from explaining Wednesday’s Treasury selloff, but it raises the next-session JGB and yen hurdle and strengthens the case that regional disinflation cannot be inferred from cheaper oil alone.

South Korea added another later Asian constraint. June consumer inflation accelerated to 3.2% from 3.1%, matching the median estimate, while core inflation held at 2.5%. Semiconductor exports nearly tripled and working-day-adjusted export growth approached 60%, strengthening the demand side of the BOK’s concern ahead of its July 16 meeting. The release occurred after the US close, so it did not cause the Treasury move. Together with the Tankan, it reinforces a regional pattern in which AI investment, currency weakness and domestic inflation limit how far cheaper global oil can lower policy rates.

Global duration thus ended with two competing forces. Europe supplied realized disinflation and less urgency for near-term tightening. Japan and South Korea supplied later evidence of stronger corporate demand, liquidity withdrawal, currency pressure and resilient domestic inflation. The US curve’s higher real yields and steeper long end fit a world where the common energy impulse is fading, but central-bank reaction functions, fiscal paths and cross-border buyer incentives remain far from uniform.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt weakened as US real yields and the dollar rose. EMB fell 0.44% to $96.02, and the Dollar Index gained 0.20% to 101.39. An intraday emerging-market currency gauge declined 0.2%, erasing its gain for 2026 at that point. The combination tightened external financial conditions even though lower oil eased the import bill for many sovereigns. The aggregate fund move reflects both Treasury duration and country risk, so the more informative signal came from currencies and specific funding plans.

Asian currencies led the pressure before Warsh spoke. The South Korean won moved toward its weakest level since 2009 as foreign investors sold a net 1.46 trillion won, or $938 million, of equities. The later Korean inflation release added a domestic policy reason for the currency and rates complex to remain sensitive. A firmer US real-rate path raises the return hurdle for local assets, while persistent Korean inflation reduces the BOK’s freedom to counter currency weakness with easier policy.

South Africa showed how sovereign and sub-sovereign funding risks could diverge. The national government planned to tap existing rand-denominated sukuk as part of its fiscal-year financing, using the local market rather than adding a new hard-currency claim. Johannesburg, by contrast, faced an estimated 2.1 billion rand unfunded budget gap for the year beginning July 1. The National Treasury said the city’s revenue projections appeared overstated relative to audited results and urged stronger collection and spending rationalization. The municipal gap is a local debt-management issue, distinct from sovereign sukuk funding.

The session therefore offered no uniform EM relief from cheaper energy. A stronger dollar, higher Treasury real yields and equity outflows weighed on external liquidity, while domestic inflation and fiscal credibility shaped country outcomes. South Africa retained access to local-currency debt channels, but Johannesburg’s budget arithmetic showed how revenue execution can constrain a sub-sovereign even when national funding continues. The main transmission remained through currency and refinancing costs, with no broad cash-spread conclusion implied by the decline in EMB.

## Cross asset

Equities supplied a concentrated growth and valuation shock. The S&P 500 slipped 0.22% to 7,483.23, but the Nasdaq 100 fell 1.54% and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index plunged 6.27%. The chip decline followed a powerful Q2 rally and renewed scrutiny of AI-linked valuations and capital spending. Broad equities did not signal recession, yet the sector most closely tied to the investment boom suffered a sharp reversal. Treasuries’ failure to rally alongside it distinguished the session from a conventional haven move.

Volatility confirmed that the selloff remained concentrated. The VIX rose only 0.85% to 16.59 and was still almost 11% lower over 1 week. Investors marked more dispersion and technology risk without paying for a systemic equity shock. That pattern helps explain why semiconductor weakness could coexist with higher real yields: the repricing challenged the distribution of AI returns, but it did not establish a broad collapse in demand or financial conditions.

Commodities leaned toward disinflation. WTI fell 1.32% to $68.58 and Brent declined 1.85% to $71.57, leaving both more than 21% lower over 1 month. Constructive US-Iran contacts and continued oil flow outweighed the weekly US inventory draws. Gold moved in the other direction, rising 1.13% to $4,068.30, though it remained 10.79% lower over 1 month. The daily bullion gain showed some demand for protection, but the longer decline remained compatible with elevated real yields.

The dollar strengthened despite lower oil and weaker technology shares, tightening the cross-border funding backdrop. Cross assets therefore complicated the rates move more than they confirmed it. Crude and euro-area inflation supported lower breakevens, and the chip selloff reduced immediate risk appetite. Stable broad equities, subdued volatility and a firmer dollar denied Treasuries a strong haven bid. The remaining rise in nominal yields was concentrated in real compensation, leaving growth resilience, policy uncertainty and long-end clearing pressure as the more durable fixed-income channels.

## Forward Market Significance

The holiday-advanced US employment block on July 2 is the nearest test of the real-yield-led selloff. June payrolls and the week-ended-June-27 claims arrive at 8:30 a.m. ET, followed by May factory orders at 10:00, before the recommended 2:00 p.m. fixed-income close. Firm hiring, wage pressure and resilient orders would extend the manufacturing signal and keep 2- through 10-year real yields under upward pressure, confirming that Wednesday’s cheaper oil and lower breakevens cannot by themselves lower the policy hurdle. Weaker employment alongside softer orders would challenge the growth explanation for the selloff and restore the possibility of belly-duration relief. The shortened session makes the persistence of the curve response, not just its initial direction, important for judging whether the move reflects policy repricing or thin holiday liquidity.

Treasury cash demand faces a separate July 2 test when $85 billion each of 4- and 8-week bills are auctioned at 11:30 a.m. ET. Both issues settle July 7, the same date as Wednesday’s $72 billion 119-day bill. Broad participation, stable short-bill yields and contained dealer awards would confirm that the current rally in 4- through 13-week cash instruments can absorb the combined holiday settlement. Abrupt cheapening, materially heavier dealer retention or stress in secured funding would challenge that interpretation and reopen the balance-sheet concern that appeared around the quarter turn. The first transmission would run through bills and collateral financing; a persistent disturbance could then reduce coupon-market liquidity during the shortened session.

Swiss June CPI at 2:30 a.m. ET and euro-area May unemployment at 5:00 on July 2 provide the next European check on the lower-inflation thesis. Soft Swiss prices, greater euro-area labor slack and oil holding near its post-war range would reinforce Wednesday’s 2.8% euro-area reading, reduce pressure on front-end European rates and confirm that the energy shock is losing breadth. Firm inflation, a tighter labor picture or renewed disruption to US-Iran negotiations would challenge the case for an extended ECB pause and transmit through Bund yields, euro inflation compensation and Treasury breakevens. The result also determines whether lower energy can ease dollar-funding pressure for import-dependent emerging markets, or whether relative-rate support for the dollar remains the dominant global channel.

section_summaries.market_read: The close raised the real-rate and term-premium hurdle despite cooler inflation inputs.
section_summaries.next_check: Persistence in long-end underperformance will show whether the higher clearing premium is durable.
section_summaries.summary: Real yields and long-end compensation drove another Treasury selloff even as oil and breakevens softened. Resilient activity, reduced Fed guidance and persistent buyer-base concerns outweighed the disinflation impulse, while selective credit stress reinforced that near-term inflation relief had not restored confidence in durable financing conditions.

section_summaries.market_read: Curve steepening shifted the selloff's center of gravity from policy risk toward duration risk.
section_summaries.next_check: Employment data and actual Fed communication frequency must confirm whether the premium persists.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Treasury losses were broad but led by the long bond, while real yields rose and breakevens edged lower. Fed-funds futures showed a tradable July hike tail even as investors built the strongest outright Treasury long in 7 months, separating near-term policy uncertainty from demand for later-cycle long-end outperformance.

section_summaries.market_read: Credit access remained available, but claim priority and liquidity mattered more than beta.
section_summaries.next_check: Brightline's control negotiations and Trans-Oil's operating assessment are the key resolution points.
section_summaries.summary: Corporate-bond funds weakened with duration and technology risk, yet private AI infrastructure debt continued to attract insurer demand. That broad funding capacity remained conditional on structure and transparency, as Brightline's unresolved creditor-control contest and Trans-Oil's headline-driven repricing exposed sharp issuer-level dispersion.

section_summaries.market_read: Global disinflation restrained inflation compensation but did not deliver broad duration relief.
section_summaries.next_check: Euro-area wages and the post-close Tankan must show whether disinflation can overcome Asian normalization pressure.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Lower euro-area inflation reduced pressure for an immediate ECB move, but the post-close Tankan showed Japanese manufacturer sentiment at its strongest since 2018 and 5-year corporate inflation expectations at a record. Fading energy inflation therefore restrained breakevens without removing Asian normalization or global term-premium floors.

section_summaries.market_read: The stronger dollar made currency and refinancing channels the dominant EM transmission.
section_summaries.next_check: Currency stability and municipal revenue execution must improve before funding pressure eases.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: EM debt weakened as higher US real yields and a firmer dollar tightened external conditions despite cheaper oil. Currency pressure, equity outflows and uneven fiscal credibility kept country outcomes differentiated, with local funding access unable to erase refinancing and revenue-execution risks.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets weakened the inflation case for higher yields but supplied little haven demand.
section_summaries.next_check: A broader volatility surge or renewed oil disruption would test the real-yield-led reading.
section_summaries.summary: A sharp semiconductor reversal and softer broad equities did not produce a Treasury haven bid, while volatility stayed subdued. Falling crude supported lower breakevens and gold gained, but the firmer dollar and stable systemic risk tone left higher real compensation as the session's dominant fixed-income channel.

section_summaries.market_read: Confirmation requires persistent curve and funding responses, not only initial headline moves.
section_summaries.next_check: Track the shortened-session curve, dealer awards and European rate response through July 2.
section_summaries.summary: Three July 2 tests will determine whether the real-yield selloff persists: the holiday-advanced US employment block, combined bill auctions and settlement demand, and European inflation and labor signals. Each separates resilient growth and policy repricing from thin-session liquidity or a renewed global inflation impulse.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

overall_summary.headline: Real yields and long-end clearing pressure overrode softer inflation signals
overall_summary.session_narrative: The completed NY close produced a real-yield-led bear steepening rather than an inflation-led selloff. Softer oil, lower euro-area inflation and a severe semiconductor reversal failed to generate a haven bid as resilient US activity, less informative Fed guidance and long-end supply concerns lifted required compensation. Selective issuer stress and a firmer dollar limited broader risk relief, while subdued volatility kept the move from becoming a systemic flight from risk.
overall_summary.summary: Treasuries cheapened across the curve as rising real yields and long-end underperformance outweighed lower breakevens and falling oil. Resilient activity and reduced Fed guidance kept the duration hurdle elevated, while credit remained open but highly selective.

watch_events.market_implication: A persistent curve response confirms policy repricing; broad weakness restores belly relief.
watch_events.market_trigger: Firm hiring, wage pressure and resilient orders keep real yields under upward pressure.
watch_events.title: US employment and factory-orders test

watch_events.market_implication: Front-end European rates and Treasury breakevens ease, reducing dollar-funding pressure.
